hero.badge: Istanbul Kultur University — Computer Engineering Department
hero.title: Erasmus Course
hero.subtitle: AI-powered course equivalence matching for Erasmus student exchange. Hybrid scoring, verification, and explainable results.

features.sectionSubtitle: From catalog ingestion to Learning Agreement — an end-to-end pipeline built for academic rigor and transparency.

aiMatching.title: AI-Powered Matching
aiMatching.desc: Hybrid scoring pipeline combining LLM semantic analysis with deterministic ECTS, metadata, and embedding-based retrieval for precise course equivalence.

smartVerification.title: Smart Verification
smartVerification.desc: Second-pass LLM audit evaluates every match for genuineness — approved, risk-flagged, or rejected with full confidence scoring.

workflow.title: Student & Coordinator Workflow
workflow.desc: Interactive course selection with ECTS tracking, coordinator review panels, override controls, and automated Learning Agreement readiness.

uniProfiles.title: Smart University Profiles
uniProfiles.desc: AI-synthesized snapshots of partner institutions — including city safety, costs of living, transportation, and English friendliness guides.

partnerUnis.title: Partner Universities
partnerUnis.desc: Multi-format catalog ingestion across Europe — consolidated PDFs, individual documents, and category-based parsing with OCR support.

explainable.title: Explainable Results
explainable.desc: Score breakdowns, matched & missing topic tags, evidence-based justification, and structural notes for every candidate match.

cta.title: Ready to match courses?
cta.subtitle: Sign in with your CATS account to get started.

form.homeExists: A home university already exists for this department.

alerts.pipeline: Course PDFs are uploaded via the Upload & Parse page. The system extracts structured data automatically using an LLM — no manual data entry needed.
alerts.modes: Each university has a configured PDF structure mode. The mode determines how the parser splits and processes the uploaded files.
alerts.prompt: Model: Gemma 4 31B-IT via Gemini API. Sends raw course text and returns structured JSON with 13 fields.
alerts.warnings: Warnings never block the pipeline. Every course is saved regardless. Warnings are shown as tags in the course table and affect matching quality scores.

step1.title: PDF Upload & Queue
step1.desc: PDFs are uploaded to the server and a job is added to a FIFO queue.

step2.title: Text Extraction
step2.desc: Raw text is extracted using PyMuPDF. OCR is applied for scanned/image-only PDFs.

step3.title: LLM Extraction
step3.desc: Raw text is sent to Gemma 4 via Gemini API to extract 13 structured fields.

step4.title: Validation
step4.desc: Parsed JSON is validated by a rule-based validator. Missing fields generate warnings.

step5.title: Embedding
step5.desc: Each course gets a text embedding for semantic similarity search.

desc.individual: One PDF per course. Entire text is sent to LLM.
desc.consolidated: Multiple courses per PDF. Parser indexes boundaries, then extracts blocks.
desc.category: PDFs uploaded per category (Core, Elective). Category label is stored.

conditions.medium: Only one of content or outcomes extracted

effects.high: Full matching quality — LLM uses both fields
effects.medium: Reduced matching — missing field scored at 50
effects.low: Poor matching — both fields scored at 50

matching.title: Batch Course Matching
matching.subtitle: All candidates scored in a single batch prompt per course
matching.onlyUnmatched: Only courses not yet matched will be processed.
matching.noJobsDesc: Select a partner university and home university, then click "Start Matching" to begin.

cancel.title: Cancel Batch Match Job?
cancel.content: Cancel matching for {{name}}? Already matched courses will be kept.

delete.title: Delete Match Job and Results?
delete.content: This will permanently remove calculated matches for {{name}}. Student-saved equivalents based on these matches will lose their connection to the matching evidence.

step1.title: Embedding Retrieval
step1.desc: Partner course embedding compared against all home university courses via pgvector cosine similarity. Top 10 most similar are retrieved.

step2.title: Deterministic Scoring
step2.desc: Each of the 10 candidates scored on 5 fast signals: ECTS credits, course title similarity, academic metadata alignment, academic level, and semester.

step3.title: Pre-filter to Top 3
step3.desc: Candidates ranked by (embedding similarity × 0.6 + deterministic average × 0.4). Only the top 3 proceed to LLM scoring.

step4.title: Batch LLM Scoring
step4.desc: All 3 candidates sent to Gemma model in a single batch call. The LLM scores content overlap, learning outcome alignment, and domain match for all 3 simultaneously and detects the academic category.

step5.title: Hybrid Fusion
step5.desc: 6 score components (3 LLM + 3 deterministic) combined with category-specific weights. Final score is a weighted sum in [0, 1].

step6.title: LLM Verification
step6.desc: After matching, a second LLM audits each match with approved/risk_flagged/rejected decisions and confidence scores.

engine.intro: The course matching engine uses a hybrid scoring approach combining vector similarity, deterministic signals, and LLM semantic analysis.
engine.item1: Academic Category Detection — The LLM classifies the partner course as technical, social, or studio_based based on dominant academic content
engine.item2: Content Score (LLM) — Topic and subject overlap between partner and home course syllabi (0-100)
engine.item3: Learning Outcomes Score (LLM) — Alignment of learning objectives (0-100)
engine.item4: Domain Score (LLM) — Broader academic field compatibility (0-100)
engine.item5: Category-Sensitive Evaluation — Structural expectations adapt to category (lab/project for technical, reading/essays for social, etc.).
engine.weightsDesc: Different academic categories use different weight profiles for the final fusion score:
engine.note: Note: The academic category is detected once per partner course and applied consistently across all 3 candidates in a batch.

verification.intro: After matching, a second LLM (Gemma 4 31B-IT) performs a post-hoc audit on the top 3 candidates as a final quality check.
verification.item1: Mutually Exclusive Approval — At most one candidate can be approved; none may be approved if no match is strong enough
verification.item2: Core Topic Coverage — Verifies whether the partner course covers the home course's essential topics (not just title similarity)
verification.item3: Content Overlap Assessment — Classifies overlap as genuine, partial, or superficial
verification.item4: Risk Flags — Identifies missing core topics, ECTS gaps, or structural mismatches
verification.item5: Confidence Scoring — Provides a confidence score (0.0-1.0) reflecting academic certainty
verification.outcome2: risk_flagged — Partially acceptable, notable gaps remain
verification.outcome3: rejected — Not academically reliable (wrong domain or superficial match)
verification.p1: Extra topics in the partner course are enrichment, not rejection reasons
verification.p2: At least 2 major missing core topics triggers rejection
verification.p4: ECTS differences alone are not rejection criteria
verification.p5: Prioritizes content and learning outcomes over course naming similarity
